title: Authorization Model
content: This document describes how Actions enforces authorization. Actions delegates
authentication to NxGN Identity and consumes JWTs — it does NOT implement its
own identity, role, or permission system.
**Key principle:** Identity tells Actions WHO the user is, WHICH tenant they
belong to, and WHETHER they are entitled. Actions decides WHAT the user can do
with actions, workflows, and configuration.
## Authorization Levels
Every API endpoint declares its required authorization level:
| Auth Level | Description | Who |
| Platform Admin | Platform-level administration of the Actions service | Members of `nxgn-platform` org with admin role (from Identity JWT) |
| Tenant Admin | Organization admin configuring workflows, compliance profiles, escalation rules, languages | Org admins (admin role in their org, from Identity JWT) |
| Actions User | Any authenticated user creating, viewing, managing, and transitioning actions | Any authenticated user with valid JWT and tenant context |
| M2M Service | Automated service creating/reading actions programmatically | Service accounts using `client_credentials` grant; identified by `client_id` |
Non-admin users calling admin-only endpoints receive `403 Forbidden` with no
information leakage (same response whether resource exists or not).
## Authorization Personas in Context
### Platform Admin
NxGN developers and platform operators. Members of the bootstrap organization
(`nxgn-platform`) with the seeded `admin` Role in Identity. They select an
organization at login — all queries are org-scoped via `tenant_id` in the JWT.
Platform admin operations in Actions are LIMITED compared to Identity. Most
administration is per-tenant. Platform admins use Identity's tenant switcher to
perform tenant-scoped operations.
| Area | Capability | Scope |
| System Health | Cross-tenant diagnostics, service health checks | Platform-wide |
| Force Transition | Force-transition an action to any state (override guards) | Org-scoped (switchable) |
| All Tenant Admin | All tenant admin capabilities (see below) | Org-scoped (switchable) |
### Tenant Admin (Organization Admin)
Customer administrators who configure workflows, compliance profiles, escalation
rules, and languages. Scoped to their own `tenant_id` — cannot see or modify
data in other organizations.
| Area | Capability | Scope |
| Workflows | Create, update, publish, list, get workflow definitions | Own org only |
| Compliance Profiles | Create, update, list, get compliance profiles | Own org only |
| Escalation Rules | Create, update, delete, list, get escalation rules | Own org only |
| Languages | Create, update, list, set default language | Own org only |
| Force Transition | Force-transition an action to any state (override guards) | Own org only |
### Actions User
Any authenticated user with a valid JWT containing `tenant_id` and `sub` claims.
No admin role required. All operations are scoped to the user's organization.
| Area | Capability | Scope |
| Actions | Create, view, list, update actions | Own org only |
| State Transitions | Transition action state (subject to guard conditions) | Own org only |
| Reassignment | Reassign action to another user | Own org only |
| Evidence | Upload, list, get evidence for an action | Own org only |
| Consultations | Create, update, list consultations | Own org only |
| Deadlines | List regulatory deadlines | Own org only |
| Audit Trail | View action audit trail | Own org only |
### M2M Service (Machine-to-Machine)
Automated services using `client_credentials` grant from Identity. M2M tokens
have `sub` set to `client_id` and carry NO `tenant_id` claim — tenant context
comes from the request body.
| Area | Capability | Scope |
| Actions | Create actions programmatically (`origin_system` derived from `client_id`) | Tenant-scoped |
| Actions | Read actions and related data | Tenant-scoped |
| Application Isolation | Read/write restricted to allowed origin systems per client | Per-client policy |
## Two Authentication Modes
**JWT Bearer tokens** — Issued by NxGN Identity. Used by the Angular web app
(via BFF), CLI, and MCP. The token contains tenant ID and actor ID claims.
Effective permissions are resolved from Identity's API and cached per-user
per-tenant. Actions validates the token signature and issuer against Identity's
JWKS endpoint (offline validation — no runtime dependency on Identity).
**API Key authentication** — Used by M2M integrations that prefer static
credentials over OAuth flows. API keys are validated against Identity's
`/api/v1/apikeys/validate` endpoint. When Identity is unavailable, API key
validation fails with 503 (unlike JWT validation which uses cached JWKS).
## Endpoint Authorization
Endpoints are split into two route groups reflecting the authorization boundary:
| Route Group | Auth Level | Who |
| `/api/v1/actions/*` | Actions User / M2M | Any authenticated user with tenant context |
| `/api/v1/admin/*` | Tenant Admin or Platform Admin | Admin role required |
## Application Isolation
M2M clients operate under an application isolation policy that restricts which
actions they can read and write based on their `origin_system`. A Capstone
service account cannot read or modify Pulse-originated actions unless explicitly
permitted.
User-interactive sessions (non-M2M) use an unrestricted policy — users can see
and manage all actions within their tenant regardless of origin system.
## Ownership Boundary
Actions owns action lifecycle concerns. Identity owns identity concerns.
| Actions Owns | Identity Owns |
| Action lifecycle and state transitions | User accounts, profiles, credentials |
| Workflow configuration and versioning | Organization/tenant management |
| Compliance profile enforcement | Role and permission management |
| Escalation rule evaluation | Session lifecycle and token issuance |
| Evidence and source link management | API key issuance and validation |
| Audit trail for action operations | Authentication (SSO, MFA, password) |
| Regulatory deadline tracking | Permission type registry |
| Consultation management | Multi-org membership |
| Per-tenant configuration (languages, workflows) | Platform roles (admin, member, viewer) |
| Application isolation policy enforcement | App entitlements |
| Origin system tracking | Client registration and secret management |
## Security Invariants
These invariants hold across all Actions endpoints and must not be violated:
1. **Tenant isolation via RLS** — All queries are tenant-scoped via PostgreSQL
Row-Level Security. A user can never see another tenant's data.
See [Multi-Tenancy](multi-tenancy.md).
2. **Admin endpoint protection** — All endpoints under `/api/v1/admin/*` require
admin role. Non-admins receive `403 Forbidden`.
3. **Force transition requires admin** — Force-transitioning an action bypasses
guard conditions and is restricted to admins only.
4. **Append-only audit trail** — The audit trail is append-only. No endpoint
allows modification or deletion of audit records. Database-level immutability
constraints prevent tampering.
5. **Optimistic concurrency** — ETag-based concurrency control prevents silent
overwrites. Stale writes return `412 Precondition Failed`. Missing `If-Match`
header returns `428 Precondition Required`.
6. **M2M origin isolation** — M2M clients are restricted to their configured
origin systems via application isolation policy.
7. **SecurityEpoch validation** — Every request validates the JWT's
`security_epoch` claim against a cached value. Tokens issued before a
security event are rejected with 401 (near-real-time token revocation).
8. **No cross-tenant queries** — There are no cross-tenant API endpoints.
Platform admins operate org-scoped via JWT `tenant_id` and switch tenants
via Identity.
